title: Fresh Fermented Vietnamese Pungent Bun Thit Nuong
description: Silky and pungent, this cold water fermented bun thit nuong features vegan-friendly ingredients and makes a great meal prep option. Perfect for serving on a cedar plank.
instructions: Soak the rice flour in cold water for at least 4 hours, or overnight.
Drain and rinse the rice flour mixture, then transfer it to a blender or food processor.
Blend the mixture until it forms a smooth, silky batter, adding water as needed.
Transfer the batter to a large bowl and add the vegan fish sauce, tofu, ginger, garlic, lemongrass, chili flakes, lime juice, palm sugar, sesame oil, coriander, and cumin.
Transfer the mixture to a cedar plank or a large dish, and cover it with a lid or plastic wrap.
Let it ferment in the refrigerator for at least 24 hours, or until it has developed a pungent aroma and a silky texture.
Once fermented, transfer the mixture to a clean bowl and shape it into small patties or balls.
Serve on a bed of shredded cabbage, with your choice of accompaniments.
Garnish with fresh cilantro or scallions, if desired.
